statement.p: Currently, an artist can sell 300 paintings every year at the price of {\$140.00} per painting. Each time he raises the price per painting by {\$20.00}, he sells 5 fewer paintings every year.

Assume he will raise the price per painting x times, then he will sell 300-5x paintings every year at the price of 140+20x dollars. His yearly income can be modeled by the equation: i=(140+20x)(300-5x) where i stands for his yearly income in dollars. If the artist wants to earn {\$47{,}200.00} per year from selling paintings, what new price should he set?
To earn {\$47{,}200.00} per year, the artist could sell his paintings at two different prices. The lower price is per painting, and the higher price is per painting.

solution.p: If the artist wants to make {\$47{,}200.00} per year, the equation becomes:
We will change the equation to standard form:
Next, we can solve this quadratic equation with the Quadratic Formula. Identify that a=-100,b=5300,c=-5200.
If the artist raises the price 1 times, the new price would be 140+20\cdot1=160 dollars per painting.
If the artist raises the price 52 times, the new price would be 140+20\cdot52=1180 dollars per painting.
To earn {\$47{,}200.00} per year, the artist could sell his paintings at two different prices. The lower price is {\$160.00} per painting, and the higher price is {\$1{,}180.00} dollars per painting.
